attractions.name: Brooks Falls
attractions.season: Bear season Jun-mid Sep; sockeye jumping the falls Jul; big fall bears Sep
attractions.cost: $100 Katmai user fee + ~$800 float plane from King Salmon
attractions.blurb: Elevated platform above a 6ft falls where brown bears catch leaping sockeye. The iconic Alaska bear photo.
attractions.tip: Falls Platform caps at 40 people with 1-hour rotations in July; sign the waitlist board the minute you land.
attractions.duration_note: long day trip from King Salmon/Homer, or 1-2 nights at Brooks Lodge

attractions.name: McNeil River Falls
attractions.season: Permit-only viewing Jun 7-Aug 25; chum run and biggest bear counts Jul
attractions.cost: $225 resident / $525 non-resident permit + $30 application + float plane
attractions.blurb: World's largest brown bear gathering - 70+ bears at one falls. ADF&G lottery permit only, 10 viewers a day.
attractions.tip: Lottery closes March 1 each year; winners camp 4 days and hike 2mi to the pad with a state biologist.
attractions.duration_note: assigned 4-day permit block, plus weather days

attractions.name: Pack Creek Bear Viewing Area
attractions.season: Open Apr 1-Sep 30; permits required and bears peak Jul 5-Aug 25
attractions.blurb: Admiralty Island brown bear viewing on a tidal flat and creek: a sand spit viewing area plus a mile-in tower.
attractions.tip: Only 24 permits a day, half held for guides. Book on Recreation.gov and expect a wet landing - rubber boots.
attractions.duration_note: full day out of Juneau including flight time

attractions.name: Anan Wildlife Observatory
attractions.season: Permits required Jul 5-Aug 25 for the pink salmon run; open 8am-6pm
attractions.cost: $10 permit + ~$300 boat or floatplane from Wrangell
attractions.blurb: Rare place where black AND brown bears fish the same creek; covered platform plus a streamside photo blind.
attractions.tip: 60 permits/day but only 12 sold to independents - they release Feb 1 at 8am AKST on Recreation.gov.
attractions.duration_note: full day from Wrangell incl. 1hr boat each way and a 0.5mi boardwalk

attractions.name: Silver Salmon Creek
attractions.season: Bears on the tide flats Jun-Sep; sockeye and silver runs Jul-Aug
attractions.cost: ~$750-950 day trip by float/wheel plane from Homer or Soldotna
attractions.blurb: Lake Clark coastal meadow where brown bears dig razor clams and fish at close range on an open beach.
attractions.tip: June bears graze sedge on the flats; late Jul-Aug they fish the creek. Beach landing, so tides set the schedule.
attractions.duration_note: day trip from Homer; overnight lodge stays available

attractions.name: Wolverine Creek & Big River Lakes
attractions.season: Sockeye stack below the falls mid-Jun to early Aug; run fades after that
attractions.cost: ~$650-800 floatplane day trip from Anchorage or Kenai
attractions.blurb: Floatplane bear viewing across Cook Inlet: brown and black bears fish a falls where sockeye jam, watched from skiffs.
attractions.tip: Closest bear fly-out to Anchorage at ~45 min each way, but strictly a Jun-Jul trip - the run dies by mid-August.
attractions.duration_note: half to full day out of Lake Hood

attractions.name: Fish Creek Wildlife Observation Site
attractions.season: Ticketed Jul 15-Sep 20; best chum/pink action mid-Jul to mid-Aug
attractions.blurb: Alaska's only road-accessible bear platform: grizzlies and black bears on chum salmon 4mi north of Hyder.
attractions.tip: Reached only by highway through Stewart, BC - bring a passport, and buy the timed ticket before you drive.
attractions.duration_note: 2-4 hrs; early morning and evening are the bear windows

attractions.name: Steep Creek Bear Viewing
attractions.season: Sockeye late Jul-early Aug, coho Sep; black bears on the creek Jul-Sep
attractions.blurb: Boardwalk beside the Mendenhall Glacier Visitor Center where black bears fish spawning sockeye and coho.
attractions.tip: Go before 9am or after 5pm when the cruise buses thin out; sockeye hit the creek the last week of July.
attractions.duration_note: 1-2 hrs, easy add-on to any Mendenhall visit

attractions.name: Chilkoot River Bear Viewing
attractions.season: Brown bears mid-Jun to Oct; sockeye and pink runs peak Jul-early Sep
attractions.blurb: Half-mile of road along the Chilkoot River between lake and inlet, with a fish weir and roadside brown bears.
attractions.tip: Park only in the marked pullouts - troopers ticket the shoulder. Evening light is best and busiest for bears.
attractions.duration_note: 1-3 hrs, 10mi drive from Haines on Lutak Road

attractions.name: Herring Cove Bear Viewing
attractions.season: Pink and chum salmon Jul-Sep pull black bears to the creek mouth
attractions.cost: Free roadside; ~$130 guided tour from Ketchikan
attractions.blurb: Creek mouth 8mi south of Ketchikan where black bears and eagles work the summer pink run - easiest port-day bears.
attractions.tip: Best on a falling tide with salmon trapped in the shallows; the hatchery run peaks the first half of August.
attractions.duration_note: 2-3 hrs including drive from downtown Ketchikan

attractions.name: Karluk Lake Bear Viewing
attractions.aka: Kodiak National Wildlife Refuge bear country
attractions.season: Sockeye Jun-Aug; bears heaviest on the creeks Jul-Sep; fly-in only
attractions.blurb: Heart of Kodiak refuge and the densest Kodiak brown bear country; floatplanes land on the lake and walk the creeks.
attractions.tip: Book a Kodiak air taxi weeks out and pad two weather days - fog scrubs more Kodiak flights than any other hub.
attractions.duration_note: full day; multi-day refuge cabin and lodge options exist

attractions.name: Alaska Chilkat Bald Eagle Preserve - Council Grounds
attractions.season: Up to 4,000 eagles Oct-early Dec on the late chum run; peak mid-November
attractions.blurb: Highway pullout with boardwalk and scopes over the Chilkat flats - the largest bald eagle gathering on earth.
attractions.tip: Alaska Bald Eagle Festival runs mid-November. Sun is low by 2pm in November, so shoot the morning side.
attractions.duration_note: 1-2 hrs; summer visitors still see 200-400 resident eagles

attractions.name: American Bald Eagle Foundation
attractions.season: Open year-round; longest hours May-Sep and during the November festival
attractions.blurb: Small Haines museum with live raptor presentations and a natural-history diorama of the Chilkat valley.
attractions.tip: The reliable eagle fix when the river run is off; combine with the preserve pullouts 20 min up the highway.

attractions.name: Point Adolphus
attractions.season: Humpbacks feed Jun-Sep; bubble-net feeding groups peak Jul-Aug
attractions.blurb: Tide-swept point on north Chichagof holding Alaska's densest summer humpback feeding; whales work near shore.
attractions.tip: Cheaper and whale-richer than a Glacier Bay day boat - book from Gustavus, not Juneau, and go on a flood tide.
attractions.duration_note: 4-6 hr boat or a full-day kayak trip from Gustavus

attractions.name: Five Finger Islands & Frederick Sound
attractions.season: Humpbacks Jun-Sep, peak Jul-Aug; orca and Steller sea lions year-round
attractions.blurb: Frederick Sound's humpback hotspot around a 1902 lighthouse; researchers track up to 100 whales on a good day.
attractions.tip: Petersburg charters are small-boat and rarely sell out like Juneau's; August is best for bubble-net groups.
attractions.duration_note: 5-8 hr charter, 41mi northwest of Petersburg

attractions.name: Whale Park
attractions.season: Shore whale watching best Sep-Jan; herring spawn spectacle Mar-Apr
attractions.blurb: Boardwalk, viewing decks and a hydrophone on Sawmill Creek Road - Sitka's free shore-based whale watching.
attractions.tip: Sitka's late-March herring spawn turns the sound milky and pulls in whales, sea lions and thousands of eagles.
attractions.duration_note: 30-60 min stop, 4mi from downtown Sitka

attractions.name: St. Lazaria Island
attractions.season: Seabirds on the cliffs May-Aug; storm-petrels return to burrows at dusk
attractions.blurb: 65-acre volcanic islet at the mouth of Sitka Sound with roughly half a million nesting seabirds.
attractions.tip: Landing is banned, so this is a circumnavigation trip. Seas get big - take motion meds and book a calm morning.
attractions.duration_note: 4-6 hr boat trip, 20mi west of Sitka

attractions.name: Icy Strait Point Whale Watching
attractions.season: Tours run May-Sep with the cruise season; whales thickest Jun-Aug
attractions.blurb: Cruise-port whale tours working the Point Adolphus waters, sold with a whale-sighting guarantee.
attractions.tip: Independent travelers can book the same boats locally in Hoonah for materially less than the ship excursion price.
attractions.duration_note: 3-4 hrs including transfer

attractions.name: South Marble Island
attractions.season: Steller sea lions year-round; tufted puffins and murres nest Jun-Aug
attractions.cost: Included in the Glacier Bay day boat (~$260)
attractions.blurb: Loud Steller sea lion haulout and tufted puffin colony - the first big wildlife stop on every Glacier Bay day boat.
attractions.tip: It comes up about 90 min into the boat run; be out on the bow deck by then, not in the cafeteria line.
attractions.duration_note: 30-60 min stop within an 8-hr Glacier Bay cruise

attractions.name: Benjamin Island Sea Lion Haulout
attractions.season: 200-1,500 Steller sea lions haul out Oct-Apr; nearly empty in summer
attractions.blurb: Rocky island in Favorite Channel 25mi northwest of Juneau - Southeast's most accessible winter sea lion haulout.
attractions.tip: This is a winter show; summer visitors find it bare. Juneau whale boats detour here Oct-Apr for humpbacks too.
attractions.duration_note: add-on to a 4-hr Lynn Canal charter

attractions.name: Cape Resurrection & Barwell Island
attractions.season: Puffins, murres and kittiwakes nest May-Aug; sea lions year-round
attractions.cost: Included in a Kenai Fjords day cruise
attractions.blurb: Sheer seabird stack at the mouth of Resurrection Bay: puffins, murres, kittiwakes and a Steller sea lion haulout.
attractions.tip: Full-day Northwestern Fjord and Aialik cruises round it; the short bay-only tours often turn back before here.
attractions.duration_note: 1-2 hrs within a 6-9 hr cruise

attractions.name: Gull Island Seabird Rookery
attractions.season: About 20,000 birds nest May-Aug; kittiwakes and common murres dominate
attractions.cost: $85-120 Kachemak Bay tour from Homer Spit
attractions.blurb: Craggy islet 8mi southeast of Homer holding ~20,000 nesting kittiwakes, common murres and both puffin species.
attractions.tip: Landing is banned (Native corporation land) - it's a circling stop; pair it with a Peterson Bay or Halibut Cove drop.
attractions.duration_note: 2-3 hr tour; often bundled with a water-taxi drop-off

attractions.name: Round Island
attractions.season: Permit access May 1-Aug 15; bull walrus haul out heaviest May-Jul
attractions.cost: $50 ADF&G permit + $1,000+ boat charter from Togiak or Dillingham
attractions.blurb: Bristol Bay island where up to 14,000 bull walrus haul out on the beaches, alongside murres, puffins and red fox.
attractions.tip: 5-day camping permits are first-come from ADF&G Dillingham; you arrange your own boat and must be self-sufficient.
attractions.duration_note: 5-day permit block; plan extra days for Bristol Bay weather

attractions.name: Beluga Point
attractions.season: Belugas chase eulachon and salmon Apr-May and Aug-Sep; bore tide year-round
attractions.blurb: Seward Highway pullout over Turnagain Arm for Cook Inlet belugas, Dall sheep on the cliffs and the bore tide.
attractions.tip: Time it about 2h15m after Anchorage low tide for the bore; belugas follow the hooligan run in mid-late May.
attractions.duration_note: 30-60 min stop, 25 min south of Anchorage

attractions.name: Windy Corner Dall Sheep Viewing
attractions.season: Dall sheep year-round; lambs late May-Jun, rams butting heads Nov-Dec
attractions.blurb: Mile 106.6 Seward Highway, the only place on earth Dall sheep are regularly seen at sea level from a road.
attractions.tip: Use the marked turnouts, never the shoulder. Sheep drop lowest to the mineral lick in late May and June.
attractions.duration_note: 15-30 min roadside stop

attractions.name: Potter Marsh Bird Sanctuary
attractions.season: Boardwalk open year-round; migration peak in May, salmon and moose Jun-Aug
attractions.blurb: Boardwalk over a Turnagain Arm wetland with arctic terns, trumpeter swans, moose and spawning salmon in July.
attractions.tip: First half of May is the migration peak; the south end of the boardwalk has the salmon channel and the moose.
attractions.duration_note: 1-2 hrs, 15 min from downtown Anchorage

attractions.name: Alaska Wildlife Conservation Center
attractions.season: Open daily year-round; longest hours mid-May to mid-Sep
attractions.blurb: 200-acre rescue center at Portage with wood bison, brown bears, moose, musk ox, lynx and caribou in big enclosures.
attractions.tip: The one guaranteed close-up of Alaska's big mammals, and it works in rain; you can drive the loop or walk it.
attractions.duration_note: 1.5-2.5 hrs, natural stop en route to Whittier or Seward

attractions.name: Kincaid Park Moose Viewing
attractions.season: Moose year-round; most visible at dawn and dusk, bulls in rut in September
attractions.blurb: 1,500-acre coastal forest at Anchorage's west end with the city's densest moose population and Cook Inlet views.
attractions.tip: Dawn or the last two hours of light. Give rutting bulls 50 yards in September and cows with calves the whole trail.
attractions.duration_note: 1-3 hrs of trails; connects to the Coastal Trail

attractions.name: Solomon Gulch Hatchery Salmon & Bear Viewing
attractions.season: Pink and chum return late Jun to mid-Aug; bears, eagles and sea lions follow
attractions.blurb: Valdez hatchery where a massive pink salmon return draws black bears, bald eagles and Steller sea lions to one spot.
attractions.tip: Peak crush is mid-July to early August; watch the sea lions work the outflow from the Dayville Road pullouts.
attractions.duration_note: 1-2 hrs, 6mi from Valdez

attractions.name: Alaganik Slough Boardwalk
attractions.season: About 5 million shorebirds pass late Apr-mid May; nesting swans and moose Jun-Jul
attractions.blurb: Boardwalk and viewing platforms on the Copper River Delta, the largest shorebird stopover on the Pacific Flyway.
attractions.tip: Copper River Delta Shorebird Festival is the first week of May. Copper River Hwy Mile 17, then 3mi down Alaganik Rd.
attractions.duration_note: 2-4 hrs including the drive out from Cordova

attractions.name: Kachemak Bay Shorebird Festival
attractions.season: Four days in early-to-mid May, timed to peak shorebird migration
attractions.blurb: Homer festival built around 100,000+ shorebirds staging on Mud Bay and Beluga Slough; walks, boats and workshops.
attractions.tip: Book Homer lodging months out - the town fills. A rising tide pushes the flocks right up to the Spit road.
attractions.duration_note: 1-3 days; core viewing is a half day

attractions.name: Creamer's Field Migratory Waterfowl Refuge
attractions.season: Cranes and geese stage Apr-May and Aug-early Sep; trails open year-round
attractions.blurb: 2,000-acre former dairy inside Fairbanks where thousands of sandhill cranes stage each August; flat loop trails.
attractions.tip: Tanana Valley Sandhill Crane Festival is late August; crane numbers in the fields peak the last two weeks.
attractions.duration_note: 1-3 hrs; the farmhouse visitor center is open summer afternoons

attractions.name: Sable Pass
attractions.season: Bus season late May-mid Sep; park road still closed at Mile 43 through 2026
attractions.blurb: Open tundra pass inside Denali's permanent wildlife closure - the park's best grizzly, caribou and Dall sheep miles.
attractions.tip: Still reachable in 2026 since buses turn at Mile 43. No off-road hiking for 5 miles - shoot from the bus.
attractions.duration_note: within a 6-9 hr Mile 43 turnaround bus trip

attractions.name: Maclaren Summit
attractions.season: Gravel road open mid-May to early Oct; caribou and berries Aug-Sep
attractions.blurb: 4,086ft alpine pass on the Denali Highway with Nelchina caribou, ptarmigan, moose and grizzly on open tundra.
attractions.tip: 130mi of gravel with no services - check your rental car's gravel-road rules. September color plus caribou is peak.
attractions.duration_note: 2-4 hrs of the 6-8 hr Denali Highway drive

attractions.name: Atigun Pass
attractions.season: Road open year-round; Dall sheep on the crags, caribou on the tundra Jun-Aug
attractions.blurb: 4,739ft Brooks Range crossing on the Dalton Highway with Dall sheep on the scree plus caribou, wolves and raptors.
attractions.tip: No services for 240mi between Coldfoot and Deadhorse - carry two spares. Sheep are high, so bring binoculars.
attractions.duration_note: 1-2 hrs within a multi-day Dalton Highway trip

attractions.name: Kaktovik Polar Bear Viewing
attractions.season: Polar bears gather ashore late Aug-Oct after whaling season; peak September
attractions.blurb: Inupiat village on the Beaufort Sea where polar bears gather ashore each fall - the most reliable wild polar bears in the US.
attractions.tip: Boat-based viewing has been shut down for years, so trips are vehicle-based. Budget two spare days for weather.
attractions.duration_note: 2-3 nights minimum given flight cancellations

attractions.name: Barter Island Airport
attractions.season: Scheduled service year-round, weather permitting; busiest in September
attractions.blurb: Gravel strip that gates every polar bear trip to Kaktovik; roughly one scheduled flight a day from Fairbanks.
attractions.tip: Fog and wind cancel flights constantly - never book a same-day onward connection out of Fairbanks.
attractions.duration_note: transit only

attractions.name: Point Barrow
attractions.season: Nesting shorebirds and snowy owls Jun-Jul; polar bears near the bone pile Sep-Nov
attractions.cost: ~$150 guided tundra tour plus airfare from Fairbanks/Anchorage
attractions.blurb: Northernmost point in the US: nesting shorebirds and snowy owls in June, polar bears at the whale bone pile in fall.
attractions.tip: You need a guide with a 4x4 for the beach track - bears use the bone pile, so do not walk out on your own.
attractions.duration_note: half day; most people fly in and out the same day

attractions.name: Safety Sound
attractions.season: Peak birding late May-mid Jun; musk ox along the road all summer
attractions.blurb: Lagoon at Mile 20 of the Nome-Council Road: eiders, loons, jaegers, Arctic terns, bluethroat and roadside musk ox.
attractions.tip: Rent a truck and drive all three Nome roads; late May to early June is the migration and display window.
attractions.duration_note: half day of the 72mi Council Road drive

attractions.name: Coffee Dome
attractions.season: Bristle-thighed curlew display window is roughly Jun 1-20 only
attractions.blurb: Tussock dome at the end of the Kougarok Road - the world's most reliable breeding site for bristle-thighed curlew.
attractions.tip: A brutal 1-2mi tussock slog uphill. Go the first two weeks of June and wear knee-high rubber boots.
attractions.duration_note: 4-6 hrs including the 72mi gravel drive each way

attractions.name: Alaska Raptor Center
attractions.season: Open daily May-Sep; reduced winter hours
attractions.blurb: Sitka rehabilitation hospital for bald eagles and owls, with a 20,000 sq ft flight-training aviary and resident birds.
attractions.tip: Eagle encounters run every 30 min in summer; the rainforest loop trail behind it is a solid rainy-day add-on.
attractions.duration_note: 1-1.5 hrs, walkable from downtown Sitka

attractions.name: Fortress of the Bear
attractions.season: Open daily; longest hours and best light May-Sep
attractions.blurb: Rescue sanctuary holding orphaned brown and black bears in converted pulp-mill tanks; close, guaranteed viewing.
attractions.tip: The fallback when a wild bear flight gets weathered out - 10 min out Sawmill Creek Road from downtown Sitka.

attractions.name: Kenai National Wildlife Refuge Visitor Center
attractions.season: Open year-round; daily in summer, reduced days in winter
attractions.blurb: Soldotna refuge headquarters with big-mammal exhibits, current bear and road reports, and short lakeside trails.
attractions.tip: Get Skilak Loop road and bear conditions here first - that loop is the peninsula's best road-accessible moose habitat.
attractions.duration_note: 45-90 min including the Keen-Eye Trail
